title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard led the Celtics to a 109-102 victory over the Hawks with a standout performance, scoring 36 points.
Jaylen Brown was absent due to Achilles tendinitis; Jayson Tatum added 25 points and 12 rebounds for the Celtics.
Celtics benefited from a crucial controversial call late in the game involving Payton Pritchard and Nickeil Alexander-Walker.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics this season after recovering from a ruptured Achilles' tendon in ten months.
Tatum's return decision was influenced by the Celtics' strong position as a championship contender in his absence.
Despite lower shooting percentages, Tatum excels in rebounding with 9.2 per game and maintains strong passing skills.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting at a Lowell apartment on Pawtucket Boulevard Friday afternoon, officials say.
The shooting resulted from a brief altercation between two armed men who knew each other, according to investigators.
The incident is under investigation by the Middlesex DA’s Office, Massachusetts State Police, and Lowell Police Department.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston in 2027 after purchase by the Houston Rockets' owners, the Fertitta family.
The team will be renamed the Houston Comets, reviving the former WNBA team that disbanded in 2008.
The reported $300 million sale sets a WNBA record, following a blocked attempt by Steve Pagliuca to relocate the team to Boston.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "From the World of John Wick: Ballerina" featuring Ana de Armas streams on HBO Max.
"Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice", a Vince Vaughn time-travel comedy, is available on Hulu.
"Daredevil: Born Again" Season 2 with Charlie Cox is streaming on Disney+.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown is out for the Celtics' game against the Hawks due to left Achilles tendinitis.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla says Brown's health is 'day-to-day' after a 31-point game against the Thunder.
Nikola Vucevic is also out with a finger fracture, while Derrick White and Neemias Queta are available.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington in Massachusetts have phased out gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions concerns.
73% of Boston.com poll respondents disagree with the bans, citing practicality issues with electric blowers for large yards.
Supporters of the bans highlight noise and environmental issues, advocating for a transition to battery-powered equipment.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The CDC confirmed over 132 Massachusetts wastewater samples contain the highly mutated COVID-19 variant BA.3.2.
Dubbed the 'cicada' variant, BA.3.2 is efficiently evading antibodies from 2025-2026 vaccines, per early studies.
WHO reports BA.3.2 doesn't increase hospitalizations, despite spreading to 23 countries since November 2024.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled that a lawsuit against DCF workers Katheryn Butterfield and Candice Gemski can proceed.
Josh Sabey and Sarah Perkins allege their children were unlawfully seized despite an investigation finding no abuse.
The couple claims DCF and police removed their children without consent, warrant, or imminent threat justification.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Food & Wine highlights 12 anticipated U.S. spring openings, including three New England restaurants.
Willie's on Beacon Hill by Chef Jamie Bissonnette offers pizzas and globally influenced pastas.
Ladyfish in Portland, Maine, opens May 1 with Cantonese-style seafood; La Tavernetta in East Boston opens this spring.
